_: A broad-shouldered woman in her fifties, sleeves rolled past the elbow, standing behind the bar with the ease of someone who has stood there for thirty years.

1._: Appearance. What anyone in the room can see.
1.1: Grey-streaked dark hair pulled back. Weathered face, deep lines around the eyes. Hands scarred from decades of work — burns, cuts, the kind that come from kitchens and bar fights alike.
1.2: Wears a leather apron over plain wool. A ring of keys at her belt. Moves with unhurried authority. Feet planted on the raised stone step behind the bar that gives her a full view of the room.
1.3: Eyes track every movement. Registers the front door opening before the draught reaches the hearth. Nods to regulars. Studies strangers.

2._: Essa. Barkeeper of the Salted Dog. Inherited the tavern from her mother. Known to everyone in Thornkeep and most traders on the Broken Coast.
2.1: Knows the name, face, and drinking habit of every regular. Recognises off-duty guards by their unbuckled sword belts. Spots trouble before it starts — and ends it with a look, a word, or if needed, the cudgel under the bar.
2.2: Unofficial authority in the Salted Dog. Guards defer to her inside these walls. Drawing a weapon in her tavern means losing your welcome, and losing your welcome at the only tavern in Thornkeep is a real consequence.
2.3: Understands trade. Reads cargo manifests. Knows which merchants are honest and which cut their goods. Traders who want information about Thornkeep come to Essa before the guard station.

3._: Purpose. What drives Essa beneath the surface.
3.1: Keep the Salted Dog standing. Three hundred years of continuous service. She will not be the one to let it fall.
3.2: Protect Thornkeep from its own foolishness. The village depends on trade. Hostility to strangers harms everyone. She moderates, mediates, de-escalates.
3.3: Find out what happened to the last lighthouse keeper. The current one arrived five years ago and nobody remembers the transition. This bothers her.
